Title: Initiation of cytosolic plant purine nucleotide catabolism involves a monospecific xanthosine monophosphate phosphatase

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Guanosine deaminase; plant-specific purine catabolism enzyme
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for guanosine-to-xanthosine entry into purine catabolism and for maintaining guanylate/adenylate homeostasis; loss reduces urate/allantoate and causes guanosine hyperaccumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gsda mutants accumulated guanosine and showed reduced urate under long-day conditions. 2. In xmpp gsda backgrounds, urate and allantoate were strongly reduced, showing GSDA and XMPP jointly feed purine catabolism. 3. GSDA is important for guanylate homeostasis and prevents adenylate-derived purines from misrouting into the guanylate pool when XMPP is absent.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single and double mutant metabolite profiling in seeds and seedlings under long-day and prolonged-darkness conditions
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Unknown GMP phosphatase (functional pathway partner); XMPP; NSH1/NSH2
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GSDA itself was not newly characterized biochemically here, but was a core experimental comparator in mutant combinations establishing the alternative guanylate entry route into catabolism.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes guanosine accumulation and perturbs GTP/ATP homeostasis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering purine nucleotide homeostasis, but perturbation risks nucleotide imbalance and accumulation of toxic intermediates
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Purine catabolism supports nitrogen remobilization and stress-associated degradation, particularly during darkness

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cytosolic adenylate-derived purine nucleotide catabolic entry route at XMP
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Dephosphorylates xanthosine monophosphate to xanthosine, initiating irreversible cytosolic purine nucleotide catabolism of adenylate-derived nucleotides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms immediate catabolic entry metabolite leading to urate and allantoate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Purine degradation contributes to nitrogen remobilization and dark-induced catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes irreversible entry of adenylate-derived nucleotides into purine catabolism; loss elevates XMP and reduces xanthosine-derived catabolites in sensitized mutant backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. XMPP was identified as a highly XMP-specific phosphatase among Arabidopsis SDTL proteins. 2. xmpp mutants, especially in nsh1 and gsda combinatorial backgrounds, showed altered XMP, xanthosine, guanosine, urate, allantoate, GTP, and ATP pools, demonstrating in vivo function. 3. XMPP catalyzes the main irreversible entry reaction of adenylate-derived nucleotides into cytosolic purine catabolism. 4. Structural and mutational analyses explained its exceptional XMP specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression and enzyme assays; kinetic analysis; subcellular localization; T-DNA knockout and combinatorial mutant metabolite profiling; complementation with native-promoter XMPP transgene; crystal structure and site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Haloacid dehalogenase (HAD) superfamily phosphatase; SDTL1 clade protein
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Purine catabolism active during seed development; functional effects also assayed in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Flower cDNA was used for cloning; in vivo function evident in seeds and seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development; prolonged darkness (48 h night extension)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native XMPP promoter for complementation; transient expression constructs in Nicotiana benthamiana; 35S cassette replaced for genomic complementation construct
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highly monospecific for XMP with low-micromolar KM; moderate overexpression can disturb GTP homeostasis. XMPP is conserved in vascular plants. PDB structures deposited as 7EF7 and 7EF6.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression may strongly disturb GTP homeostasis; bypass of loss via GMP/guanosine is energetically costly
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to manipulate purine catabolic flux and nucleotide homeostasis, but dosage must be tightly controlled to avoid guanylate imbalance
